varieties.name: Vedic (Standard Commercial Type)
varieties.origin: Selected Indian genotype, commercially distributed in Australia
varieties.traits: Compact branching shrub 75-100cm height, pale green leaves, small lantern-like husks, bright red berries when mature
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate root yield 300-500kg dried roots per hectare
varieties.availability: Happy Valley Seeds, Mudbrick Herb Cottage (QLD, NSW, VIC, SA, ACT), All Rare Herbs, Shaman Australis Botanicals, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Australian garden centers

varieties.name: CIMAP-Pratap
varieties.origin: CSIR-Central Institute of Medicinal & Aromatic Plants, Lucknow, India, 2011
varieties.traits: Tall type (180cm height), dark green leaves (8.10cm average), green stem with red berries, robust growth
varieties.yield_notes: Highest dry root yield variety at 3495kg/ha (34.95 quintals/ha), high fresh and dry leaf yields 539kg and 87kg per hectare

varieties.name: Poshita
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP, Lucknow, India, 2001. Name means 'well-nourished'
varieties.traits: Tall morphotype, vigorous growth, large robust plants, profuse branching
varieties.yield_notes: High dry root yield variety, third highest at 2500-3000kg/ha, consistently productive

varieties.name: NMITLI-118
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP, Lucknow, India. New chemotype breeding line
varieties.traits: Perennial type, distinct chemotype with specialized withanolide profile, uniform growth characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high root yield 2200-2800kg/ha, bred for quality over quantity
varieties.availability: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, research institutions, specialized pharmaceutical crop suppliers

varieties.name: NMITLI-101
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP, Lucknow, India. High-yielding chemotype
varieties.traits: Perennial type, strong growth habit, good disease resistance
varieties.yield_notes: Second highest yielding variety at 3000-3200kg/ha dry root yield
varieties.availability: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, medicinal plant research centers, specialized importers

varieties.name: CIMAP-Chetak
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP, Lucknow, India. Annual type breeding line
varieties.traits: Annual type variety, shorter crop duration, rapid growth, good branching
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield 1800-2400kg/ha, faster crop turnaround

varieties.name: CIM-Pushti
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP, Hyderabad, India, 2018. Withanolide-A rich variety
varieties.traits: Annual type, highly wavy sub-coriaceous light green leaves, semi-erect strong culm, yellow-orange mature berries, good uniformity
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 900-1000 quintals roots per hectare (9000-10000kg/ha)
varieties.availability: CSIR-CIMAP Hyderabad, specialized medicinal plant suppliers

varieties.name: Arka Ashwagandha
varieties.origin: ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research, Bangalore, India
varieties.traits: Annual type, disease-resistant breeding line, uniform plant characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Dry root yield around 1200kg/ha (12 quintals/ha)

varieties.name: Jawahar Ashwagandha-20 (JA-20)
varieties.origin: Jawaharlal Nehru Krishi Vishwavidyalaya, Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh, India, 1998
varieties.traits: Short stature (most amenable for high-density planting), compact growth habit, space-efficient
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield 400-600kg/ha, suited for intensive planting systems
varieties.availability: Jawaharlal Nehru Krishi Vishwavidyalaya, Madhya Pradesh agricultural suppliers, may be available through specialized seed importers

varieties.name: Jawahar Ashwagandha-134 (JA-134)
varieties.origin: National Botanical Research Institute / Jawaharlal Nehru Krishi Vishwavidyalaya, Madhya Pradesh, India
varieties.traits: Medium-sized plants, annual type, good branching structure
varieties.yield_notes: Average dry root yield 650kg/ha, selected from JA-20 through pedigree selection
varieties.availability: Research institutions in Madhya Pradesh, specialized seed suppliers

varieties.name: Nagori (Nagauri) 🏆 🌏 INDIGENOUS/HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Traditional variety from Nagaur district, Rajasthan, India. 100+ years cultivation history. Supreme among all Ashwagandha varieties according to classical Ayurvedic texts.
varieties.traits: Short morphotype with orange berries, compact growth 60-90cm height, traditional landrace characteristics, locally adapted over generations
varieties.yield_notes: Lower root yield than improved varieties (400-700kg/ha) but commands premium price due to superior quality

varieties.name: Traditional Kerala Wild Type (Amukkuram) 🏆 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Indigenous to tropical monsoon climates and southern India. Found growing wild in dry regions. Traditional collection from natural populations.
varieties.traits: Variable morphology from wild populations, 60-120cm height, adapted to local tropical monsoon climates conditions over millennia, genetic diversity in wild populations
varieties.yield_notes: Variable yield from wild collection, typically 300-600kg/ha from cultivated wild-type selections

varieties.name: CIMAP-Pratap
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, 2011
varieties.traits: Tall type 180cm, dark green leaves, green stem with red berries, robust perennial
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yielding variety 3495kg/ha dry roots

varieties.name: Poshita
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, 2001
varieties.traits: Tall morphotype, vigorous growth, large robust plants, red berries
varieties.yield_notes: High yield 2500-3000kg/ha, third highest yielding variety

varieties.name: NMITLI-118
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, specialized chemotype
varieties.traits: Perennial type, distinct chemotype, pharmaceutical-grade breeding line
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate-high 2200-2800kg/ha, quality-focused breeding
varieties.availability: CSIR-CIMAP research centers, pharmaceutical crop suppliers, tropical monsoon climates Ayurvedic pharmaceutical companies, NMPB-RCFC Southern Region for research purposes

varieties.name: NMITLI-101
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, high-yielding chemotype
varieties.traits: Perennial type, strong growth, disease resistant
varieties.yield_notes: Second highest at 3000-3200kg/ha

varieties.name: CIM-Pushti
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP Hyderabad, 2018
varieties.traits: Annual type, light green wavy leaves, yellow-orange berries, leaf blight tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: 9000-10000kg/ha excellent yield
varieties.availability: CSIR-CIMAP Hyderabad center, tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities, NMPB-RCFC Southern Region, medicinal plant suppliers in southern India

varieties.name: Arka Ashwagandha
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR Bangalore, Karnataka (neighboring state to tropical monsoon climates)
varieties.traits: Annual type, disease-resistant breeding, uniform characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: 1200kg/ha moderate yield
varieties.availability: ICAR-IIHR Bangalore, Karnataka State Seeds Corporation, tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities, NMPB-RCFC Southern Region, horticultural nurseries in tropical monsoon climates and Karnataka

varieties.name: Rakshita
varieties.origin: CSIR-CIMAP Lucknow, India. High-yielding variety
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated variety, consistent performance, good uniformity
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding variety 2200-2800kg/ha

varieties.name: Jawahar Ashwagandha-20 (JA-20)
varieties.origin: Jawaharlal Nehru Krishi Vishwavidyalaya, Madhya Pradesh, 1998
varieties.traits: Short stature, high-density planting suitable, compact growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate 400-600kg/ha, intensive planting increases total yield

varieties.name: Jawahar Ashwagandha-134 (JA-134)
varieties.origin: National Botanical Research Institute / JNKVV, Madhya Pradesh
varieties.traits: Medium-sized annual plants, good branching
varieties.yield_notes: 650kg/ha average, selected from JA-20

varieties.name: Raj Vijay Ashwagandha-100 (RVA-100)
varieties.origin: University of Horticultural Sciences / Jawaharlal Nehru Krishi Vishwavidyalaya, Madhya Pradesh, India
varieties.traits: Annual type variety, good vigor, consistent growth characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield 500-700kg/ha (5-7 quintals/ha), large individual root size

varieties.name: Gujarat Anand Ashwagandha-1 (GAA-1) / Anand Withania-1 (AWS-1)
varieties.origin: Anand Agricultural University (AAU), Gujarat, India
varieties.traits: Annual type variety, adapted to semi-arid conditions, robust growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good yield 600-800kg/ha

varieties.name: Vallabh Ashwagandha-1 (DWS-132)
varieties.origin: Sardarkrushinagar Dantiwada Agricultural University or research station, India
varieties.traits: Improved variety, consistent characteristics, good adaptability
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield 500-700kg/ha

varieties.name: WSR
varieties.origin: CSIR-Regional Research Laboratory, Jammu, India. Northern hill region breeding
varieties.traits: Adapted to hill conditions, can tolerate temperature variations, robust variety
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield adapted to regional conditions

varieties.name: 1. Nagori (Heirloom) - Traditional supreme quality, cultural significance, excellent for Ayurvedic use
